quote_of_day.text:
>>20259
The Pajeet Father emasculates and humiliates his son in every way possible just to affirm his own masculinity.

The Pajeet Son just bends over and takes it, because he has no choice.
quote_of_day.name: Paggi Slayer

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of casteist remarks moved into the board's Leading section, sparking a row with multiple squalls of replies. The system intensified, drawing parallel lines of argument between casteist bigotry and debate troubles.

A drizzle of personal guilt and familial strife fell in the Discourse section, as one anon expressed regret over their parents' ongoing fights, attributing blame to their own academic shortcomings.

Nearby, a front of North-South India tensions developed, fueled by caste and cinematic depictions. A scattered downpour of true crime reports and personal confessions occurred in the Notices section.

A graphic scenario of alleged matricide for 'uninterrupted sex' emerged, alongside an anon expressing desire to be a biological female, seeking to experience life in a different capacity.

articles.title: Casteist remarks spark row on Indiachan
articles.body:
Dalit Front, a user on Indiachan, posted a commentary on June 23rd, drawing parallels between casteist attitudes and reactions to interracial violence.

The original post expressed frustration with debating individuals who hold bigoted views, citing a hypothetical scenario where someone might joke about a white person killing an Indian family.

A respondent, Anon, replied with a threat, stating that Dalit Front would "face the wall" and referencing Modi. Another anon chimed in, calling Dalit Front a "low IQ chamar", a slur.

Dalit Front responded to the threats, questioning the need for "dhamki" (a type of ultimatum or warning) and referencing the presence of Ambedkar's image on BJP and RSS materials.

They also mentioned that communities like Jaats, Yadavs, and Gujjars are not part of the "bhraminchal system", implying a complex social dynamic. The exchange highlights tensions surrounding caste and identity on Indiachan, with users taking different stances on the issues raised.

One respondent drew a comparison between the experiences of dalits and the reactions of some Indians to violence against them. The thread showcases the divisive nature of discussions on Indiachan, where users often engage in heated exchanges.

The tone of the conversation turned confrontational, with personal attacks and threats. Dalit Front's initial post seemed to aim at highlighting the perceived hypocrisy and double standards in some individuals' reactions to caste-based and racist remarks.

articles.title: OP blames self for parents' fights
articles.body:
Anon posted about their parents fighting almost daily, expressing hatred for their father and his side of the family, and self-blame for not being able to change the situation through studying harder.

One anon suggested that the fights would stop if the father knew how to 'hatefuck and satisfy' the mother, implying sexual frustration as the cause.

Another anon asked for more information about the OP's situation, inquiring about their age, caste, and the reason for the fights.

Anon responses ranged from brutal and offensive, suggesting the OP subjugate their parents, to seemingly more empathetic, with one asking if the father was an alcoholic.

Some anons discussed the role of money in household fights, with one stating it's mostly the reason in 'gareeb households'.

The thread also included some off-topic comments, with one anon noting that newer iterations of the board have a different user base than earlier ones, now filled with 'pessimistic and cuck shit'.

Others made provocative statements, such as suggesting the OP leave if they have a healthy family or recommending they open a Reddit account.

The OP's feelings of guilt and frustration were met with a variety of reactions, from dismissive and hostile to somewhat sympathetic. No clear resolution or supportive discussion emerged.

articles.title: Caste and Cinematic Depictions Fuel North-South India Tensions
articles.body:
An anonymous poster opened the discussion by criticizing AASIjeet, suggesting that being 'cuck' is part of his DNA and listing several perceived grievances, including an obsession with certain topics and a perceived disdain for Hinduism.

One respondent, Dalit Front, referenced what they termed as 'Kathmulla propaganda' and brought up the movie Kedarnath, which they claimed promotes a Muslim boy and Hindu girl love story with class differences, suggesting it portrays school and college girls being targeted by Muslim men.

Another poster countered the initial criticism, stating that southern India, particularly Tamil Nadu, produces a significant number of engineering graduates and chess players, and hosts major projects like Google's headquarters and ISRO's launch station.

The discussion quickly turned to the portrayal of Hindu-Muslim relationships in cinema, with one poster accusing southern filmmakers of creating content that depicts Hindu girls being 'liberated' from orthodox families and adopting Muslim practices.

The exchange also touched on the role of communists in the south, with one poster alleging that communist Telugus masquerade as Tamils and control the government and movie industry, sparking accusations and denials from other users.

The conversation was marked by several personal attacks and insults, with one poster labeling others as 'northie chamars' and 'hindi niggers,' while another suggested that northern Indians are envious of the south's economic and educational achievements.

The thread also saw users debating the influence of caste and regional identity on Indian politics and society, with some asserting that southern India is not immune to problems like communism and others highlighting what they see as northern India's shortcomings.

articles.title: Priti Kumari allegedly murders mother-in-law for 'uninterrupted sex' with father-in-law
articles.body:
Anon posted about Priti Kumari, a 24-year-old from Jharkhand, who allegedly murdered her 55-year-old mother-in-law, Kulsi Devi, to have uninterrupted sex with her 60-year-old father-in-law, Daulat Saw.

Priti's husband was working in Hyderabad and sending her money every month. Another anon mentioned that in some rural areas of central India, people from marginalized groups force their daughters into prostitution.

A respondent named Paggi Slayer shared a personal anecdote about their own family dynamics, describing their father as emasculating and humiliating.

Anon also referenced data from NFHS, stating that women from rural areas have more sexual partners over their lifetime than urban women, and that lower-caste women, especially Dalit women, are more sexually mobile.

articles.title: Anon expresses desire to be biological female
articles.body:
Anon posted a desire to be a biological female without identifying as trans, citing a wish to wear 'sexy outfits.' Another anon asked what was stopping the OP, suggesting they 'KYS' and joking about a 50% chance of being a woman in the next life.

A respondent brought up menstruation and hygiene, questioning if the OP was sure about their desire. A separate anon criticized the discussion, stating 'No one called her gobar, says a lot about you anons.'
